name: Phoenix Loans
description_short: Private money lender specializing in fix-and-flip, rental property, and construction loans for real estate investors across select U.S. markets.
description_long:
Phoenix Loans was founded in 2009 during the real estate crisis with the explicit mission to streamline borrowing for distressed property purchases and real estate investment. The company positions itself as a private money lender focused on serving professional and individual real estate investors rather than traditional homebuyers. Over 15+ years of operation, they report funding 421 loans totaling $120,000 in volume across their borrower base.

Phoenix Loans offers three primary loan products: Fix & Flip Loans for distressed property acquisition and renovation, Rental Property Loans for investment properties, and Construction Loans for development projects. Their stated process is designed for speed, emphasizing that investors can "fund your next project in a matter of minutes." The company operates in five states: Arizona, Colorado, Kentucky, Ohio, and Texas, with plans to expand into additional markets. Borrowers apply through an online form and work directly with their team for loan requests and account management.

The company differentiates itself through speed of funding, simplicity of process, and specialization in investor financing rather than consumer mortgages. Their marketing emphasizes a four-step process and positions themselves as "capital partners" in property revitalization. Customer testimonials highlight fast turnaround times and professional service, though these are limited in scope and self-selected.

Phoenix Loans is fundamentally a private money/hard money lender, not a traditional mortgage company. As such, borrowers should expect higher interest rates and fees than conventional financing, shorter loan terms, and lending decisions based primarily on property value rather than credit scores. The company's geographic limitation to five states and lack of detailed rate/term information on their website means borrowers must contact them directly for pricing. The testimonials, while positive, are minimal and unverified.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Specializes in fix-and-flip and distressed property loans, serving investors underserved by traditional lenders
Fast funding process advertised as completing in minutes, addressing real estate investor time constraints
Operates across five states (Arizona, Colorado, Kentucky, Ohio, Texas) with stated plans for expansion
Established company with 15+ years of operating history since 2009
Offers three distinct loan products tailored to investor needs: fix-and-flip, rental property, and construction loans
Simple online application process with dedicated team support for loan requests
Reports over 421 loans funded with positive customer testimonials on service speed
cons: No interest rates, fees, or loan terms displayed on website; borrowers must contact for pricing
Geographic limitation to only five states reduces accessibility for investors in most of the U.S.
Limited transparency on lending criteria, credit score requirements, or loan-to-value ratios
As private money lender, rates and terms typically significantly higher than conventional mortgages
Customer testimonials are minimal, unverified, and self-selected; no independent reviews provided
best_for: Real estate investors seeking fix-and-flip financing for distressed properties
Rental property investors needing non-traditional lending in Arizona, Colorado, Kentucky, Ohio, or Texas
Construction project developers requiring short-term capital for development loans
Borrowers with good property collateral but credit profiles that may not qualify for conventional financing
diagnosis: Phoenix Loans is best for experienced real estate investors seeking non-traditional financing for fix-and-flip, rental property, or construction projects in their five-state service area. The main caveat is that as a private money lender, borrowers should expect significantly higher rates and fees than conventional mortgages, and should carefully compare pricing with other hard money lenders before committing, as rate information is not disclosed upfront.
services: Fix & Flip Loans for distressed property acquisition and renovation
Rental Property Loans for investment properties
Loan Request application through online form
Direct team consultation for loan requests
Lending services in Arizona, Colorado, Kentucky, Ohio, and Texas
Private money lending for real estate investors
Capital partnership for property revitalization projects
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
